title: genista cephalantha var. gomarica
intro: Genista cephalantha var. gomarica is a rare and lesser-known variant of the Genista plant species, commonly known as a shrub or subshrub, which is native to the Iberian Peninsula. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Genista cephalantha var. gomarica are said to include the potential to support cardiovascular health and immune system function, as well as exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal properties. In herbal preparations, this plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
